title: Build the Pulse Runner browser arcade game

intake.mode_reason: The repository has no product app or game direction, so an isolated static browser game is the smallest safe outcome.

questions.question: Where can a game live without changing the framework proof fixture?
questions.conclusion: A self-contained game directory and opt-in package scripts preserve all existing framework and proof behavior.

assumptions.statement: A polished score-chasing arcade game is acceptable because the user did not specify genre, theme, or technology.
Local browser play is the delivery target; public hosting is out of scope.

synthesis.outcome: Pulse Runner is a polished, responsive browser game with a complete replayable loop.
requirements: Provide score, energy, combo, pause, game-over, and restart states.
Keep game simulation deterministic and independently testable.
Present a responsive neon arcade interface with readable instructions.
constraints: Do not alter or weaken the repository proof fixture or its negative cases.
Do not require external services or runtime dependencies.

goal_spec.objective: Deliver the Pulse Runner game defined by this frozen contract.
goal_spec.mechanism: Beads parent and child work items
goal_spec.fallback_reason: The user requested the workflow skill but did not explicitly authorize persistent goal creation.

acceptance.behavior: The player can steer, collect pulses, build a combo, lose energy on hazards, and reach game over.
The browser exposes start, pause, resume, game-over, and restart as usable states with current score and energy.
Keyboard and touch controls are available and the layout remains usable at desktop and mobile widths.
The final game has a coherent, readable neon arcade presentation with clear hierarchy and interaction feedback.

checks.claim_scope: Deterministic simulation, steering, collision, scoring, combo, energy depletion, and terminal-state rules.
The real static candidate starts, responds to keyboard input, pauses, resumes, restarts, exposes touch controls, and renders correctly at desktop and mobile viewports.
